The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE NORMAN LLOYD PETERSEN REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #4, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/18/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The proof of service filed on 8/27/24 claims that notice was mailed on 6/28/24 for this hearing on 10/28/24, which would have been impossible. This hearing was continued from 8/28/24 as of 8/26/24. (2) Notice must also have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) The e-filed petition appears to be incomplete. No courtesy copy has been provided to the examiner, as required by LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B., so it has not been fully reviewed online yet. Please provide courtesy copies of a complete petition and any other papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 12/6/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SUSIE BAYSANGER JONES
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/23/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proof of service has been filed. (Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.]) (2) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (3) If Decedent's brother, Mart Baysanger, died on 4/4/22, he post-deceased. Will an estate not be opened for him? If not, Probate Code Section 13100 affidavits will need to be filed. (4) The Corrected I&A filed on 3/4/24 shows assets to be all cash in a Morgan Stanley retirement account, but they were not. Non-cash assets must be appraised by the probate referee. (5) The "accounting" is not at all adequate. [See Probate Code Section 1060, et seq.] (6) If all funds were deposited in an interest-bearing account, why does the accounting not show any receipts of interest? (7) How did the successor administrator determine that the decedent did not have an interest in the real property after all? (8) If the estate consists of the decedent's sole and separate property, why would 50% go to Ida Mae Jones? Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner before 12/13/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a probate referee HAS BEEN appointed. Frank Mahoney III was appointed on 8/25/20 and remained so as of 1/10/24.) Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] In addition to the requirements of CRC 2.111 and 7.102, all pleadings must also show the name and representative capacity, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., John Jones, Executor. "Petitioner" or "Respondent" alone is not sufficient. [LRSF 14.15 A.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ZOILA REGINA JEREZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. (The problem with the wording "no issue surviving" in the initial petition is that that could be interpreted to mean that a predeceased child had issue who subsequently died. It would have been clearer had item 8 just shown "no issue" by those predeceased children's names.) For future notices of hearing, please print out the instructions for remote appearance and attach the instructions to the Notice of Hearing. There is no need to mail the instructions separately.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SANH VAN LUONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/23/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Issues posted in the tentative ruling for the 8/5/24 hearing still have not been fully addressed. (2) Who are or were the parents of the niblings listed in item 8? Are they all children of Jacki Truong? (3) If Jacki Truong is the decedent's only sibling, and he has disclaimed, are all of his issue listed in item 9? (What is the purpose of the other people's signature on the disclaimer?) (4) Item 3f of the petition is incomplete. (5) Item 7 of the petition is incomplete (6) Item 8 of the petition must list the heirs of the predeceased spouse. New deadline is 12/13/24 for providing the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EUGENE RICHARD BULLETT
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/25/24. The following issues must be cleared: (1) Petition #8 must set forth whether each of the predeceased relatives was survived by issues. The examiner is unable to review whether notice was given to all known heirs at law without the allegation. If not all heirs were known and disclosed at the outset, the personal representative will likely need to petition for entitlement before any distribution. LRSF 14.35.H. (2) Proposed order has not been submitted. LRSF 14.7.A. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
